zh.lifeSummary: 當James Patoun於大約1535年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾丁波時，他的父親George Seton 6th Lord Seton29歲，母親Elizabeth Hay26歲。他於1557年2月17日與Janet Stevenson在英國蘇格蘭斯林史爾聖 尼尼安斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1605年2月13日在他的家鄉享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Patoun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Patton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Peatáin, see Payton .

English: from either of two places called Patton in Shropshire and Westmorland. The Shropshire placename is recorded as Peattingtune in 901 and derives from an Old English personal name Peatta + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Westmorland placename is recorded as Patun in 1086 and derives from Old English pæth ‘path, track’ or an Old English personal name Patta + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Patton
meanings: English, northern Irish, and Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scottish personal name Patun, Paton, an Old French diminutive of Pat(e), a pet form of Patrick. Compare Patrick and Pattinson .

History: The American general George Patton (1885–1945) was born in San Gabriel, CA, into a family with a long military tradition. His earliest American ancestor, Robert Patton, had emigrated from Scotland to VA c. 1770.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Patoun was born about 1535, in Edinburgh, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, George Seton 6th Lord Seton, was 29 and his mother, Elizabeth Hay, was 26. He married Janet Stevenson on 17 February 1557, in Saint Ninians, Stirlingshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 13 February 1605, in his hometown, at the age of 71.

ko.lifeSummary: James Patoun(이)가 대략 1535년 영국 스코틀랜드 에딘버그h에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Seton 6th Lord Seton(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Hay(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1557년 2월 17일 영국 스코틀랜드 스티를링실르 세인트 니니누스에서 Janet Stevenson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1605년 2월 13일 그의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.
